questions.scenario: Maya's local code reads a dev secret successfully, but Cloud Run fails in production. The code is the same. What likely changed?

options.text: ADC found a different identity locally than the service account used by Cloud Run.
Secret Manager stops working only in production.
Cloud Run cannot use service accounts.
The project ID can never affect credentials.

questions.explanation: ADC is credential discovery. Local code may use a human credential while Cloud Run uses the attached runtime service account.

questions.scenario: A developer wants local behavior to be closer to production without downloading a long-lived key. Which approach fits the article?

options.text: Use approved service account impersonation where appropriate.
Copy the production service account key into the repo.
Give every developer project Owner forever.

questions.explanation: Impersonation can let a developer test as a service account without distributing a long-lived service account key.

questions.scenario: Which questions belong in local ADC review?

options.text: Is the test using a user identity or an impersonated service account?
Can we skip production identity review because local success proves everything?

questions.explanation: Local identity evidence matters. Local success does not prove production service account access.
